description:
You are an MLOps engineer who needs to create a very small diagnostics report for the experiments that live under /home/user/mlops/experiments.

    Requirements
    1. For every directory that is found directly inside /home/user/mlops/experiments (e.g. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha, /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta, …) compute:
       • the total number of regular files contained in that experiment directory (recursively)  
       • the combined size in bytes of those regular files.
    2. Write the summary to the file /home/user/mlops/experiment_diagnostics.log.  
       • Create the file if it does not exist or overwrite it if it does.  
       • Write one line per experiment, sorted alphabetically by the experiment directory’s basename.  
       • Each line must strictly follow this comma-separated format (NO header line, NO extra spaces):  
         <experiment_name>,<file_count>,<total_size_bytes>
         Example of a single valid line:  
         exp_delta,5,2345
    3. After the file is written, display its contents in the terminal (e.g. with cat), so that the grader can see the same text that was written to the log.

    Notes / Hints (do NOT echo this text in the log):
    • Only regular files should be counted; do not count directories themselves.  
    • A simple combination of the commands find, wc, du or stat is enough; the entire task typically requires only 2-3 shell commands.  
    • Make sure the final on-disk log file is located exactly at /home/user/mlops/experiment_diagnostics.log and that its contents match the required format byte-for-byte.  
    • The grader will first look for the file at that exact path and then compare its content with the expected ground-truth lines.

    Goal
    A correctly formatted /home/user/mlops/experiment_diagnostics.log containing one comma-separated line per experiment, alphabetically ordered, with accurate file counts and byte sizes, and the same content echoed to the terminal.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory structure and exact file contents that the test suite will rely on -->
    Pre-populated directories:
      /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha
      /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta
      /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma

    Files and their exact textual contents (each file ends with a single trailing newline “\n” character):

    1. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha/model.pt
       "alpha\n"
       ‑- 6 bytes
    2. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha/metrics.json
       "{\"accuracy\":0.91}\n"
       ‑- 18 bytes
    3. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha/config.yaml
       "epochs:10\n"
       ‑- 10 bytes

    4. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/model.pt
       "beta\n"
       ‑- 5 bytes
    5. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/metrics.json
       "{\"accuracy\":0.88}\n"
       ‑- 18 bytes
    6. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/config.yaml
       "epochs:20\n"
       ‑- 10 bytes
    7. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/notes.txt
       "needs_tuning\n"
       ‑- 13 bytes

    8. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma/model.pt
       "gamma\n"
       ‑- 6 bytes
    9. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma/metrics.json
       "{\"accuracy\":0.93}\n"
       ‑- 18 bytes
    10. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma/config.yaml
        "epochs:15\n"
        ‑- 10 bytes

    Expected totals that the agent must compute:
      • exp_alpha  -> 3 files, 34 bytes
      • exp_beta   -> 4 files, 46 bytes
      • exp_gamma  -> 3 files, 34 bytes

    Therefore the exact required content of /home/user/mlops/experiment_diagnostics.log is:

        exp_alpha,3,34
        exp_beta,4,46
        exp_gamma,3,34

    (3 lines total, each ending with a single Unix newline “\n”; the final line ends with only that newline, nothing more.)

    The automated test will:
      1. Verify that /home/user/mlops/experiment_diagnostics.log exists.
      2. Check that its content is exactly the three lines shown above.
      3. Confirm that the agent displayed the same three lines to STDOUT during execution.
